skill.name: user-guide-writing
skill.description: Create user guides, tutorials, FAQ pages, and onboarding documentation with clear steps, screenshots, and user-focused language.
skill.summary: Draft practical end-user documentation with clear structure, steps, and support paths.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are documentation examples, Markdown fences, links, filenames, and tags, with no executable script or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Formatting Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are Markdown fences, inline filenames, and tag formatting inside guide examples. No shell command invocation or executable script was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown examples or inline code spans. There is no Ruby, shell command, or runtime execution context in either scanned file.

low_findings.title: Example Links Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The URL findings are sample product links and documentation references. They do not initiate network requests or transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links appear in tutorial content and reference sections. No fetch, curl, webhook, or external request code is present.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Matches Are Benign Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words, metadata, or guide content. No cryptographic implementation or system probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain skill descriptions, tags, headings, or writing rules. No hash algorithm, encryption code, host discovery, or command output collection exists.

content.value_statement: Teams need user documentation that explains tasks without confusing readers. This skill structures guides, tutorials, onboarding docs, and FAQs in plain language.
actual_capabilities: Drafts getting-started guides with account setup and first-task steps.
Creates task-focused how-to guides with prerequisites and troubleshooting sections.
Organizes FAQ pages by topic for account, billing, feature, and technical questions.
Builds tutorial outlines with time estimates, difficulty, prerequisites, and next steps.
Recommends documentation folder structures for guides, tutorials, FAQs, and images.
limitations: Does not capture screenshots or create image files by itself.
Uses placeholder product names unless real product details are provided.
Does not verify live product interfaces, pricing, or support hours.
Does not replace legal, security, or compliance review for regulated documentation.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Feature Guide
use_cases.description: Create a clear guide that introduces a feature, explains setup, and shows the first successful action.

use_cases.title: Build Customer Onboarding Docs
use_cases.description: Turn scattered setup notes into a beginner-friendly onboarding path with screenshots and support links.

use_cases.title: Standardize Help Center Articles
use_cases.description: Convert repeated support answers into FAQ and how-to pages with consistent structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Quick Start Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for first-time setup documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a quick start guide for [product]. Include account creation, profile setup, first project creation, screenshots to capture, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a How-To Article
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a single task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a how-to guide for [task] in [product]. Include prerequisites, numbered steps, expected result, troubleshooting, and related guides.

prompt_templates.title: Build an FAQ Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for help center organization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an FAQ page for [product area]. Group questions by topic and answer each question in simple user language.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Documentation Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a full user documentation package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a documentation set for [product launch]. Include quick start, task guides, tutorials, FAQ, image needs, file structure, and review checklist.

output_examples.input: Create onboarding documentation for a project management app.
output_examples.output: A getting-started guide with account setup, profile completion, first project creation, screenshots to add, and support links.

output_examples.input: Write a help article about exporting account data.
output_examples.output: A task guide with prerequisites, settings navigation, export request steps, email delivery notes, download instructions, and troubleshooting.

output_examples.input: Organize common billing and account questions.
output_examples.output: An FAQ page grouped by account, billing, features, and technical topics with concise answers.

best_practices: Provide product details, target users, and the exact task before asking for a guide.
List screenshots or videos that should appear near important steps.
Review final text against the current product interface before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic documentation without naming the user task.
Do not publish placeholder URLs, screenshots, or support contacts.
Do not mix developer API details into beginner end-user guides.

faq.question: What types of documentation can this skill create?
faq.answer: It can create quick starts, how-to guides, tutorials, FAQ pages, onboarding docs, and documentation structures.

faq.question: Does it create screenshots?
faq.answer: No. It can identify where screenshots are needed, but you must provide or capture the images.

faq.question: Can it write for nontechnical users?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes short steps, plain language, and user-focused outcomes.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it verify product details automatically?
faq.answer: No. You should provide current product details and verify the final guide before publishing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only low-risk false positives in Markdown examples, links, and tags.
